framework.cuda_note: Experiments run on Apple M1 CPU; GPU optional for larger networks
framework.reasoning: Paper explicitly uses d3rlpy (PyTorch-based) for CQL; NumPy/SciPy for Ising EMVS; PyMC/NumPyro for MCMC. No GPU requirement stated.

train.py.role: CLI entrypoint for full Q-Ising pipeline

tensor_flows.name: Dynamic Ising Forward Pass (node i, period t)
pseudocode: INPUT: y_prev [N], action a_t (int), node i (int), theta_i [4 + |N_i|]
treatment_self = 1 if a_t == i else 0
neighbor_treated = 1 if a_t in N_i else 0
peer_sum = sum(gamma_{k, m_j} * y_prev[j] for j in N_i)
eta = beta_0_k + beta_1_k * treatment_self + beta_2_k * y_prev[i] + beta_3_k * neighbor_treated + peer_sum
prob = sigmoid(eta)   → scalar in (0,1)
OUTPUT: adoption probability for node i

tensor_flows.name: State Construction (period t)
pseudocode: INPUT: panel history up to t, theta_hat
For each node i: compute eta_i with a_t = empty (zero out treatment indicators)
l_hat_0_i = sigmoid(eta_i_counterfactual)   → [N]
For each bin k: l_bar_0_k = mean(l_hat_0_i for i in B_k)   → [K]
For each bin k: y_bar_{k} = mean(y_prev[i] for i in B_k)   → [K]
s_t = concat(l_bar_0, y_bar)   → [2K]
OUTPUT: state s_t [2K]

tensor_flows.name: CQL Q-Function Forward Pass
pseudocode: INPUT: s [2K] float32
x = BatchNorm(s)   → [2K]
x = Linear(2K, 256) -> ReLU -> Dropout(0.3)   → [256]
x = Linear(256, 256) -> ReLU -> Dropout(0.3)  → [256]
q_values = Linear(256, K)   → [K]
OUTPUT: Q(s, b) for all b in [K]   → [K]

tensor_flows.name: Ensemble Policy (majority vote)
pseudocode: INPUT: s [2K], P ensemble agents, P state representations s^(p) [2K]
For each agent p: a_p = argmax_k Q_p(s^(p), k)   → scalar
votes = count(a_p == k for each k in [K])   → [K]
OUTPUT: argmax_k votes[k]

entrypoints.description: Run full Q-Ising pipeline on a network
cli_args: --ensemble (flag): use MCMC ensemble instead of EMVS

entrypoints.description: Compare multiple policies on a trained simulator
cli_args: --policies (list): random degree lir degree_bin plain_dqn q_ising

entrypoints.description: Generate observational panel from SIS simulator

risk_assessment.description: EMVS implementation is non-trivial: requires weighted penalized logistic regression with spike-and-slab EM updates. The exact EM update equations are not in the paper; must follow Rockova & George (2014).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement simplified version: fix spike-slab inclusion probs, solve L1-regularized logistic regression. Make EMVSSolver an abstract base class so it can be swapped.

risk_assessment.description: HMC/NUTS sampling via PyMC may have convergence issues for high-N networks with many coupling parameters.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add NUTS diagnostics (R-hat, ESS). Default to EMVS for large N; expose mcmc as opt-in flag.

risk_assessment.description: SIR confidence 0.65 for implementation_assumptions: EMVS inner solver details are underspecified.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Design IsingFitter as abstract class with plug-in solvers; add TODO comments at low-confidence points.

risk_assessment.description: Indian microfinance village adjacency data requires access to Banerjee et al. 2013 dataset (publicly available on Harvard Dataverse but download script needed).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Include data/download_villages.py script pointing to Harvard Dataverse URL.

risk_assessment.description: d3rlpy API may differ across versions; CQL hyperparameter names change between major releases.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Pin d3rlpy version; wrap CQL in thin adapter class.

risk_assessment.description: Ensemble with 20 agents x 60s = ~20 min training; acceptable on M1 but slow on CPU-only servers.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add --n-agents flag; default to 5 for quick runs, 20 for full replication.
